name: act
description: Run GitHub Actions workflows locally using Docker — test CI pipelines without pushing to GitHub, with fast feedback loops
when_to_use: Testing and debugging GitHub Actions workflows locally before pushing, iterating on CI configuration

install.command: brew install act || go install github.com/nektos/act@latest

name: GitHub CLI
description: Official GitHub CLI — create PRs, manage issues, trigger workflows, review checks, and interact with GitHub APIs from the terminal
when_to_use: Creating pull requests, managing issues, triggering CI workflows, checking run status, managing releases

install.command: brew install gh || winget install GitHub.cli

name: git-cliff
description: Generate changelogs from git history using conventional commits — customizable templates with keep-a-changelog and custom formats
when_to_use: Before releases, automating CHANGELOG.md generation, enforcing changelog consistency in CI

install.command: brew install git-cliff || cargo install git-cliff

name: commitlint
description: Lint commit messages against conventional commit format — enforces structured commit history for automated changelog generation and semantic versioning
when_to_use: As a git hook or CI check to enforce conventional commit messages across the team
what_it_finds: Non-conforming commit messages, missing type prefix, incorrect format

name: docker build
description: Build container images from a Dockerfile — package applications with dependencies for consistent deployment across environments
when_to_use: Building container images for deployment, creating reproducible build artifacts, CI image pipelines

install.command: brew install --cask docker || curl -fsSL https://get.docker.com | sh

name: docker compose
description: Define and run multi-container applications — orchestrate services, networks, and volumes from a declarative YAML file
when_to_use: Running multi-service stacks locally, integration testing with real dependencies, development environments

install.command: Included with Docker Desktop or install docker-compose-plugin

name: just
description: Command runner similar to make but focused on running project-specific commands — no build system complexity, just recipes in a justfile
when_to_use: Defining project commands (build, test, lint, deploy), replacing Makefiles for task running without build graph overhead

install.command: brew install just || cargo install just

name: GNU Make
description: Classic build automation tool — define targets with dependencies in a Makefile for reproducible project workflows
when_to_use: Standard project automation, defining build/test/deploy targets, when a Makefile already exists

install.command: Pre-installed on most Unix systems || brew install make || apt install make

name: Earthly
description: CI/CD framework combining Dockerfiles and Makefiles — reproducible builds that run identically on local machines and CI with layer caching
when_to_use: When you want containerized, reproducible CI pipelines that work identically locally and in CI — Earthfile syntax combines Docker + Make

name: Dagger
description: Programmable CI/CD engine that runs pipelines in containers — write pipelines in Go, Python, or TypeScript instead of YAML
when_to_use: When you want to write CI pipelines as real code instead of YAML, with local execution and CI portability

install.command: brew install dagger/tap/dagger || curl -fsSL https://dl.dagger.io/dagger/install.sh | sh

name: Buildkite Agent
description: CI/CD agent that runs build jobs on your own infrastructure — pipelines defined in YAML with dynamic step generation
when_to_use: Self-hosted CI/CD with Buildkite's pipeline orchestration, artifact management, and parallelism control

name: CircleCI CLI
description: Validate and run CircleCI pipelines locally — config validation, job execution in Docker, and orb management from the terminal
when_to_use: Validating CircleCI config before pushing, running jobs locally for debugging, managing CircleCI orbs

name: Jenkins CLI
description: Command-line interface for Jenkins — manage jobs, trigger builds, view console output, and administer Jenkins from the terminal
when_to_use: Scripting Jenkins interactions, triggering builds from CLI, managing Jenkins jobs and plugins programmatically

install.command: Download jenkins-cli.jar from your Jenkins instance: curl -O https://<jenkins>/jnlpJars/jenkins-cli.jar

name: Drone CLI
description: Container-native CI/CD platform — lightweight, Docker-based pipelines defined in YAML with minimal configuration overhead
when_to_use: Self-hosted CI/CD with container-native execution, simpler alternative to Jenkins for Docker-based workflows

install.command: brew install drone-cli || curl -L https://github.com/harness/drone-cli/releases/latest/download/drone_linux_amd64.tar.gz | tar zx && mv drone /usr/local/bin/

name: release-please
description: Google's automated release tool — creates release PRs with changelogs from conventional commits and handles version bumping
when_to_use: Automating releases in GitHub repos, generating changelogs from conventional commits, managing semantic versioning in CI
